entries.name: Hollow Jester
entries.habitat: Abandoned carnivals · Urban ruins · Underground tunnels
entries.lore: Once entertainers in a forgotten carnival, the Jesters were the first to be touched by the rift. Their painted grins are now seared into the bone, and their laughter precedes them by several seconds — a delayed echo from somewhere just on the wrong side of reality. Survivors who hear the laugh and see no jester are advised to assume one is already behind them.
entries.tips: Do not be fooled by their stagger. They lunge from a crouch with twice the reach of any survivor weapon. Keep moving, and never reload while one is within ten metres.
entries.behaviour: Hunts in groups of 2–3. Will mirror a survivor's movement for several seconds before committing to a lunge. Drops to all fours when wounded and accelerates.
entries.firstSighted: The Big Top, Year One — three days after the rift opened.

entries.name: Frost Surgeon
entries.lore: The Frost Surgeons have one purpose left in their machinery: to operate. They will pursue any wounded survivor across miles of terrain to "complete the procedure" — and the outcome is never in the patient's favour. Recovered logs from St. Aldred's Hospital suggest at least nine were active before the wing fell silent.
entries.tips: Their orb projectile arcs slightly. Strafe perpendicular and close the distance fast — they fold in melee. Burn the cryo-coil on the back of their neck to disable freeze attacks.
entries.behaviour: Maintains a 12 m engagement distance. Tracks bleeding targets preferentially — survivors with active wound debuffs are prioritised over healthy ones.
entries.firstSighted: St. Aldred's Hospital, third quarantine wing.

entries.name: Veil Stalker
entries.habitat: Forests at dusk · Rooftops · Sewer networks
entries.lore: No one has seen the face beneath the mask. Survivors who claim to have lifted it are never seen again. The Veil Stalkers do not roar; they whisper your name from a direction you can never quite locate. Field analysis suggests their masks are not worn — they have grown into the bone.
entries.tips: They flank. If one is in front of you, two more are behind. Light up the area with a flare or torch and they break engagement immediately.
entries.behaviour: Pack hunters that operate in triangular formation. Will not engage a target carrying an active light source unless they outnumber 3-to-1. Disappear into shadow if line of sight is broken for more than 2 s.
entries.firstSighted: Greyfen Pine forest, north ridge — first reported by Ranger Hale.

entries.name: Tunnel Wretch
entries.lore: Buried alive when the rift opened, these miners adapted. Their lungs no longer require air — only the dark wet smell of stone. They are slow, but the tunnels they dig are everywhere. Some survivors swear they can hear the distant tap of pickaxes through bedrock from over a kilometre away.
entries.tips: Slow but tanky. Kite them in a circle and pump rifle rounds into the back of their head. Their amber eyes are weak points — three clean shots will down one even at full HP.
entries.behaviour: Solitary or in mining-team trios. Will tunnel through soft terrain to reach a target if blocked. Becomes enraged and ignores armour when reduced below 30% HP.

entries.name: Husk Watcher
entries.lore: Stand perfectly still and the Husk Watcher will not move. Look away and turn back, and it has somehow taken three steps closer. They never blink. They never need to. The crows that ring their fields do not eat the grain — they sing for the watcher when prey approaches.
entries.tips: Statues. They cannot chase you, but they will gut you if you walk past unaware. Burn them from range, or never break eye contact while passing.
entries.behaviour: Quantum sentinel — only animates while not under direct observation. Will reposition behind cover if all witnesses are blinded or look away. Treats fire as the only credible threat.
entries.firstSighted: Halloway Farm, harvest season — a survey team returned with two dead.

entries.name: Drowned Diver
entries.lore: Their suits are still pressurised from the dive that killed them. Inside, the water remembers. The helmets emit a steady ping at exactly 28 Hz — the same frequency the rift gave off the night it opened. Marine salvage crews report the divers walk along the seabed for miles before surfacing in unexpected places.
entries.tips: Wet ground means they can swap positions. Stay on dry land. Aim for the helmet seal — one cracked porthole and the pressure inside does most of the work for you.
entries.behaviour: Surfaces from any standing water within 50 m of a target. Maintains medium range, lobbing pressurised brine projectiles. Will re-submerge to reposition rather than fight in melee.
entries.firstSighted: Pier 14, Old Harbour — washed ashore on the third night.

entries.name: Lizard Mule
entries.habitat: Anomaly fields · Cinder wastes · Open glasslands
entries.lore: Bred in the anomaly fields from a strain that didn't quite finish dying, the lizard mule carries cargo through air the colonies wouldn't breathe. They are calm in train and lethal in defence — a wounded mule will gore the thing that hit it long after the rest of the pack has fled. Surface caravans budget one mule for every two survivors; survivors who lose theirs do not return.
entries.tips: Heavy hide deflects ranged fire. Lead them in a wide circle to draw the two-strike combo, then dump into the soft throat between charges. Never reload inside their reach — the tail counter alone kills unarmoured survivors in one swing.
entries.behaviour: Solitary or paired. Idles at rest until line-of-sight is closed under 14 m, then enters a heavy stomp-and-charge pattern. Roots in place on hit and counter-swings within 0.4 s. Flees only when reduced below 15% HP.
entries.firstSighted: Salt-Pan Corridor, Year Three — three caravans walked in, none came out.

entries.name: Skeleton Swordsman
entries.habitat: Cathedral ruins · Sunken graveyards · Anomaly tombs
entries.lore: Field analysts insist the skeletons of the Ruined Cathedral are not undead in the strict sense — only metal and bone re-knitted by something in the rift that finds the shape of a swordsman aesthetically agreeable. The blade is bonded to the wrist. The wrist is not happy about it.
entries.tips: Their guard is brittle. Two clean blunt strikes to the ribcage will fold the rig. Avoid trading swings — the bonded blade ignores parries.
entries.behaviour: Idles in formation around graves or altars. Wakes when a survivor enters a 10 m radius and walks directly toward them, swinging on contact.
abilities: Re-form (returns to idle if not killed cleanly)

entries.name: Skeleton Axe-Bearer
entries.habitat: Cathedral ruins · Anomaly tombs · Old battlefields
entries.lore: The axe-bearers are the older skeletons, the ones the cathedral kept after the battles ended. Their swings start slow and end somewhere on the other side of you. Survivors describe the impact as "less like being hit and more like the floor deciding to stop existing."
entries.tips: Heavier hits, slower wind-up. Sidestep the overhead and counter into the spine. Two clean blunt strikes still fold the rig.
entries.behaviour: Idles like the swordsman; engages with a single heavy overhead chop on a 2.4 s cadence. Will not chase past its anchor altar.
abilities: Re-form (returns to anchor if not killed cleanly)

entries.name: Tide Caller
entries.habitat: Sunken shrines · Pier ruins · Flooded basilicas
entries.lore: She does not move. The water moves. Pilgrims who reached the shrine and returned describe a tall figure in green-grey robes whose face is always angled away — and a tide that follows their gaze. The 28 Hz ping the Drowned Divers emit is louder near her. Three salvage teams have died standing perfectly still.
entries.tips: Static target. Bring electric or salt-iron. Do not stand in standing water — that's how she lands the tidal pulse. The pulse telegraphs ~1.5 s with a low hum; break line of sight or run for a dry plinth.
entries.behaviour: Stationary. Emits a 12 m radius tidal pulse every 6 s that staggers anyone in contact with standing water. Summons a Drowned Diver pair at 75% and 25% HP.
entries.firstSighted: The Drowned Basilica, low tide — a single salvor returned, refused to speak of the figure.
